questions.question: A stock's price increased from $15 to $20. What is the rate of change?
If a company's profit is directly proportional to its sales, and profit is $4000 when sales are $20000, what is the profit when sales are $30000?
A tax of $0.08 is added to each dollar spent on a purchase. If an item costs $25, what is the total cost?
If $500 is invested at an annual interest rate of $4\%$, how much will it be worth after one year?
A savings account earns interest at a rate of $3\%$ per annum. If $800 is deposited, how much interest will be earned in one year?
The distance between two cities is $240$ miles. If a car travels at $40$ miles per hour, how long will the trip take?
A company's cost is directly proportional to the number of products it manufactures. If it costs $150 to produce 50 items, what is the cost of producing 100 items?
A water tank can hold $3600$ gallons. If $1200$ gallons of water are already in the tank, what percentage of the tank is filled?

A bakery sells a total of $250$ loaves of bread per day. They sell a combination of whole wheat and white bread. If the ratio of whole wheat to white bread is $3:5$, how many loaves of each type of bread are sold?
A car rental company charges a base fee of $\$20$ plus an additional $\$0.25$ per mile driven. If a customer rents a car for a day and drives it $120$ miles, what is the total cost?

teacher_rules: Review the concept of unit rates
Focus on direct and inverse variation
